2026-04-23T08:49:35.5435970Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5436548Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5436999Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5437372Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5437684Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5437921Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5438080Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5438264Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5438436Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5438669Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5438870Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5439160Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T08:49:35.5439427Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-04-23T08:54:36.4303250Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T08:50:28.690986569Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58579,\"title\":\"[DependencyMap] Docs: README lacks service-specific key concepts and code examples\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/dependencymap/Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit c6e44de (PR #58562) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap` 1.0.0-beta.2 was just released (dependency upgrades to Azure.Core 1.53.0 and Azure.ResourceManager 1.14.0). The package has a rich set of resources and operations (including `DependencyMapResource`, `DependencyMapDiscoverySourceResource`, and long-running operations like `ExportDependencies`, `GetDependencyViewForAllMachines`, `GetConnectionsForProcessOnFocusedMachine`) but the README contains only boilerplate.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Key concepts section** is entirely generic — links only to general Azure SDK docs with no description of `DependencyMapResource`, `DependencyMapDiscoverySourceResource`, or what the service does\\n- **Examples section** contains no code snippets — only a link to a generic samples gallery; there are no `Samples/` test files backing any inline code\\n- **No `Samples/` directory** exists under `tests/` — missing snippet-backed examples for CRUD operations and key operations such as `ExportDependencies` and `GetDependencyViewForAllMachines`\\n- **Stale \\u0026#34;Documentation\\u0026#34; section** that is not part of the standard README template (should be folded into Getting Started or removed)\\n- **API reference link** still uses the old `docs.microsoft.com` URL instead of `learn.microsoft.com`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/dependencymap/Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the generic **Key concepts** section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### DependencyMap\\n\\nA `DependencyMapResource` represents an Azure Dependency Map instance scoped to a resource group. It stores the topology of discovered dependencies between machines and processes. Key operations include:\\n\\n- **Create / Update** — provision a new dependency map or update its tags\\n- **Export Dependencies** — run a long-running operation to export the dependency graph\\n- **Get Dependency View** — retrieve a dependency view for all machines or a focused machine\\n- **Get Connections** — query network connections for a process on a focused machine\\n\\n### DiscoverySource\\n\\nA `DependencyMapDiscoverySourceResource` is a child resource that describes a source from which dependency data is collected (for example, an Azure Monitor workspace). You can create, update, and delete discovery sources on a dependency map.\\n```\\n\\nRemove the standalone `## Documentation` section (the links it contains are duplicated in Getting Started).\\n\\nReplace the generic **Examples** section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n- [Create a dependency map](#create-a-dependency-map)\\n- [Get a dependency view for all machines](#get-a-dependency-view-for-all-machines)\\n\\n### Create a dependency map\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a dependency view for all machines\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFix the API reference URL in Getting Started:\\n\\n- Old: `(docs.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- New: `(learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/dependencymap/Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap/tests/Samples/Sample1_DependencyMapCrud.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_DependencyMapCrud : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task CreateDependencyMapAsync()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DependencyMap_CreateOrUpdate\\n            // Authenticate and get a subscription\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            // Get or create a resource group\\n            string rgName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(rgName);\\n\\n            // Create or update a DependencyMap\\n            string mapName = \\u0026#34;myDependencyMap\\u0026#34;;\\n            DependencyMapData data = new DependencyMapData(AzureLocation.EastUS);\\n            ArmOperation lro = await resourceGroup\\n                .GetDependencyMaps()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, mapName, data);\\n            DependencyMapResource dependencyMap = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created DependencyMap: {dependencyMap.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task GetDependencyViewForAllMachinesAsync()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DependencyMap_GetDependencyViewForAllMachines\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n            DependencyMapResource dependencyMap = await resourceGroup.GetDependencyMaps().GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myDependencyMap\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            // Request a dependency view for all machines in the map\\n            GetDependencyViewForAllMachinesContent content = new GetDependencyViewForAllMachinesContent(\\n                DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddHours(-1),\\n                DateTimeOffset.UtcNow);\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro =\\n                await dependencyMap.GetDependencyViewForAllMachinesAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, content);\\n            GetDependencyViewForAllMachinesOperationResult result = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Dependency view status: {result.Status}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nSnippet-to-README mapping:\\n| `#region` tag | README placeholder location |\\n|---|---|\\n| `Snippet:DependencyMap_CreateOrUpdate` | Under `### Create a dependency map` heading |\\n| `Snippet:DependencyMap_GetDependencyViewForAllMachines` | Under `### Get a dependency view for all machines` heading |\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nCurrent README is missing or needs improvement in the following sections:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Install the package | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with service-specific content (see Step 1) |\\n| Examples | ❌ No code | Add snippet placeholders (see Step 1) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Minimal but present | No change required |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Documentation (non-standard section) | ⚠️ Not in template | Remove or merge into Getting Started |\\n\\nReference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/dependencymap/Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/dependencymap/Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 dependencymap` — injects snippet code from the new sample file into README.md placeholder blocks; check `git diff` to confirm README updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 dependencymap` — regenerates public API listing files (run if any public types changed)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/dependencymap/Azure.ResourceManager.DependencyMap/` — ensures consistent code formatting\\n\\n\\n\\n##
